machinery_genes.quote: The ΔvscN1-ΔvscN2 mutant strain derived from the RIMD2210633 clinical isolate contains double deletions of genes vscN1 and vscN1, which encode structural components of the T3SS1 and T3SS2 secretion apparatuses, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: Nguyen et al 2020, mSphere; same RIMD 2210633 strain. Source sentence has a typo ('vscN1 and vscN1') but unambiguously names vscN1 as the structural component of the T3SS1 apparatus. vscN1 = VP1668 in the genome annotation; ATPase by Ysc/SctN homology. Park et al 2004 (PMID 15501799) independently showed deletion of TTSS1 apparatus genes in RIMD2210633 abolishes cytotoxicity.

machinery_genes.quote: The POR strains contain mutations in the vcrD1 and vcrD2 genes that are an essential component of the inner membrane ring of the needle's basal structure in T3SS1 and T3SS2, respectively.
machinery_genes.note: Calder et al 2014, FEMS Microbiol Lett; POR strains derived directly from clinical RIMD 2210633. vcrD1 names the T3SS1 inner-membrane basal-body ring (LcrD/SctV family export apparatus).

machinery_genes.quote: the vscF gene encodes the needle protein of T3SS1 in V. parahaemolyticus
machinery_genes.note: Lian et al 2021, Front Cell Infect Microbiol. Paper also reports VscF is homologous to the MxiH needle protein. vscF = VP1694 in other annotations (locus tag not used as the gene field per curation rules).

machinery_genes.quote: In our previous study, vscI was found to be critical for effector translocation.
machinery_genes.note: Lian et al 2021. Lower-confidence machinery call: the paper ties vscI to T3SS1 effector translocation and treats it in parallel with the needle gene vscF (both deletion mutants reduce translocation), consistent with an SctI inner-rod role by Ysc homology, but this paper does not explicitly use the word 'apparatus' or 'inner rod' for vscI. Included because it is named as a T3SS1 translocation-machinery gene, not an effector or chaperone.

machinery_genes.quote: VP1657 (VopB1) and VP1656 (VopD1), which share sequence similarity with Pseudomonas genes popB (38%) and popD (36%), respectively, are essential for translocation of T3SS1 effectors into host cells... Thus, we conclude that VopB1 and VopD1 are essential components of the translocon.
machinery_genes.note: Shimohata et al 2012, Can J Microbiol. VopB1 = VP1657, popB homolog. Translocon proteins form the host-membrane pore and are counted as apparatus (not effectors) per the T3SS brief, despite being secreted.

machinery_genes.quote: VP1657 (VopB1) and VP1656 (VopD1), which share sequence similarity with Pseudomonas genes popB (38%) and popD (36%), respectively, are essential for translocation of T3SS1 effectors into host cells... Thus, we conclude that VopB1 and VopD1 are essential components of the translocon.
machinery_genes.note: Shimohata et al 2012, Can J Microbiol. VopD1 = VP1656, popD homolog. Park et al 2004 (PMID 15501799) independently showed 'VopD... was secreted in a TTSS1-dependent manner' (YopD homolog), corroborating its T3SS1 association.

notes: T3SS-1 is the chromosome-1 (Ysc/Sct-type) injectisome of V. parahaemolyticus RIMD 2210633, first identified by Makino et al 2003 (genome) and shown functional by Park et al 2004. The literature consistently describes the whole apparatus as the vp1656-vp1697 gene cluster (~30 Yersinia-Ysc-homologous apparatus genes), but most papers refer to individual structural genes only by VP locus tag plus a functional label (e.g. Nydam 2014: VP1668 ATPase, VP1670 ruler, VP1691 inner rod, VP1696 outer-membrane secretin, VP1690/VP1695 MS-ring), NOT by a Vsc/Vcr gene name. Per the curation rule that records gene NAMES with a verbatim naming quote, only the genes for which a paper actually states a gene name as a T3SS1 apparatus component are listed: the ATPase (VscN1), the export-apparatus inner-membrane ring (VcrD1), the needle (VscF), an inner-rod/needle-associated translocation gene (VscI, lower confidence), and the two translocon proteins (VopB1/VopD1). Status = PARTIAL because the secretin (VscC1/VP1696), inner ring (VscJ), export gate (VscR/S/T/U), C-ring (VscQ), and ruler (VscP/VP1670) are present in the genome and named in the literature by locus tag, but I found no published sentence naming them by a defensible Vsc/Vcr gene NAME as a component of this system, so they are intentionally not listed. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: T3SS1 effectors (VopS/VP1686, VopQ/VPA0450 context effector, VPA0226, VP1680, Vp1659/VopR), the ExsACDE regulators (exsA/VP1699, exsD/VP1698, exsC/VP1701, exsE/VP1702), and chaperones. Vp1659 (PMID 20418402) is an LcrV-like 'T3SS1-associated' protein reported as apparatus-related but is a secreted/translocated tip-like protein and was not included to stay conservative. All quotes verbatim from the cited abstracts/full texts; all PMIDs and DOIs confirmed via get_article_metadata.
